text: Transformers use scaled dot-product attention to relate tokens across the full sequence.
The multi-head attention mechanism projects queries, keys, and values into multiple subspaces.
Positional encodings inject sequence order information because attention is permutation-invariant.
The feed-forward sublayer in each transformer block applies two linear projections with a ReLU in between.
Layer normalisation is applied before each sublayer in the pre-norm variant used by most modern LLMs.
BERT uses bidirectional attention; GPT uses causal (left-to-right) attention masking.
The softmax in attention converts raw scores to a probability distribution over key positions.
Flash Attention reorders operations to reduce HBM reads/writes and avoid materialising the full attention matrix.
Rotary position embeddings (RoPE) encode relative positions directly into the query-key dot product.
Grouped-query attention shares key/value heads across multiple query heads to reduce memory bandwidth.
Vector databases store high-dimensional embeddings and support approximate nearest-neighbour queries.
HNSW builds a multi-layer graph where each node is connected to its approximate nearest neighbours.
IVF (Inverted File Index) clusters vectors into Voronoi cells and searches only the nearest clusters.
Product quantisation compresses vectors by splitting them into sub-vectors and quantising each independently.
Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, and Milvus are popular managed or open-source vector database solutions.
sqlite-vec adds vector similarity search to SQLite via a loadable extension with a virtual table interface.
Recall@k measures what fraction of the true top-k neighbours are returned by an ANN index.
Cosine similarity normalises for magnitude, while L2 (Euclidean) distance preserves it.
Hybrid search combines dense vector retrieval with sparse BM25 keyword matching.
Filtering in vector DBs can be done pre-filter (restrict the candidate set) or post-filter (filter results).
pyproject.toml is the modern standard for Python project metadata, replacing setup.py.
uv is a fast Python package and project manager written in Rust that replaces pip and virtualenv.
The [dependency-groups] table in pyproject.toml declares dev-only dependencies like pytest.
A lock file pins every transitive dependency to an exact version for reproducible installs.
PEP 517 defines a build-system interface so tools like uv or build can create wheels without setuptools.
Editable installs (pip install -e) create a link to the source tree so code changes take effect immediately.
The [project.scripts] table maps console-script entry points to Python callables.
Semantic versioning (SemVer) uses MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH to communicate breaking changes.
Virtual environments isolate project dependencies to avoid version conflicts across projects.
Hatch, PDM, Poetry, and uv are modern Python project managers that handle venvs and packaging.
RAG grounds LLM responses in retrieved documents to reduce hallucination and improve factuality.
A RAG pipeline has three stages: indexing documents, retrieving relevant chunks, and generating a response.
Chunking strategy matters: too small loses context, too large dilutes relevance signals.
Re-ranking retrieved candidates with a cross-encoder improves precision before passing to the LLM.
HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embeddings) embeds a generated answer and uses it as the query vector.
Contextual compression trims each retrieved chunk to only the sentences relevant to the query.
Multi-hop RAG retrieves supporting documents iteratively, each retrieval conditioned on prior results.
The context window limits how many retrieved chunks can be passed to the LLM at once.
Lost-in-the-middle: LLMs tend to ignore information in the middle of a long context window.
RAGAS is an evaluation framework for RAG pipelines measuring faithfulness, answer relevancy, and context precision.
SQLite is a serverless, file-based relational database embedded directly in the application process.
WAL (Write-Ahead Logging) mode allows concurrent reads while a write is in progress.
SQLite uses dynamic typing: values carry their own type, and columns have type affinity, not strict types.
The EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN statement shows how SQLite will execute a query and whether it uses an index.
FTS5 is SQLite's full-text search extension supporting BM25 ranking and prefix queries.
SQLite supports JSON functions like json_extract and the -> operator for querying JSON columns.
Transactions in SQLite are ACID-compliant; wrapping bulk inserts in a transaction is much faster.
The sqlite3 Python module ships with the standard library and provides a DB-API 2.0 interface.
Loadable extensions let you add custom functions, collations, and virtual tables to SQLite at runtime.
STRICT tables, added in SQLite 3.37, enforce column type constraints like a traditional RDBMS.
